franchisor.principal_address: 8000 Tower, Suite 700, 8331 Norman Center Drive, Bloomington, MN 55437

concept.description: DQ Treat — quick-service retail selling DQ soft-serve, treat products, beverages, and limited approved food items

initial_franchise_fee.balance_refundable_conditions: 4 specified conditions: withdraw before construction, training failure, no site within 90 days, no construction within 180 days

royalty.gross_sales_definition: Total revenues and receipts from all products sold, cash/credit/gift card, including non-DQ branded, excluding sales taxes and gift card sale revenue
royalty.exceptions: May be reduced for specialty captive-venue locations. Conversions and relocations may have different rates.

advertising_fee.rate: 5%-6% of Gross Sales (most products); up to 6% for Orange Julius branded
advertising_fee.management_fee: 7% of SPF payments (not on SPF above 3%)

term.renewal_years: Shorter of 15 years or remaining lease term
term.renewal_conditions: 3-6 months notice; sign then-current agreement; modernize; good standing; right to premises; pay renewal fee; sign release

territory.encroachment_risk: ADQ and affiliates may compete in your area. Territory operators not bound by ADQ site clearance guidelines.

noncompete.during_term: No involvement in QSR serving hamburgers (no alcohol) or business with >10% revenue from ice cream/yogurt/frozen custard/fruit beverages/soft-serve/frozen treats
noncompete.post_term_radius: 500 meters of authorized location (Street) or same building/venue (Captive-venue)
state_overrides: North Dakota: enforceable only under certain conditions

dispute_resolution.arbitration_venue: Minneapolis, MN or mutually agreed place
dispute_resolution.litigation_venue: Federal District Court for District of Minnesota or Hennepin County District Court, Minneapolis
dispute_resolution.governing_law: Law of state where authorized location is situated
state_overrides: California: venue restrictions may not apply
Illinois: venue outside IL void (except arbitration)
Minnesota: no out-of-state litigation, no jury waiver
North Dakota: arbitration at nearest AAA office
Rhode Island: forum restriction void for RI Act claims
Washington: arbitration in WA or mutually agreed place

termination.by_franchisee: For material breach after 30-day notice; effective 60 days after notice

by_franchisor_cure_periods.24_hours: Defaults impairing goodwill or public health/safety
by_franchisor_cure_periods.10_days: Failure to provide reports or pay amounts due

termination.non_curable: Loss of premises, failure to reopen, abandonment, insolvency, conviction, intentional underreporting, 3rd default in 12 months, OFAC designation
termination.termination_fee: 2x last 12 months continuing license fee (or prorated formulas)

transfer.conditions: Transferee qualifies, fee paid, amounts owed paid, compliance, guarantee, release, training, sign then-current agreement (materially different possible), facility improvements

item19_fpr.verbatim_no_fpr: We do not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance or the past financial performance of company-owned or franchised outlets.

item20_outlets.gag_clause_verbatim: In some instances, during the last three fiscal years, current and former franchisees have signed provisions restricting their ability to speak openly about their experience with ADQ.

training.required_attendees: Designated manager + 1 assistant manager (first location); designated manager only (ARD)

supply_chain.entity: Unified Supply Chain, Inc. (USCI), IDQ subsidiary
supply_chain.cost_to_establish_pct: 80%-90% of establishment cost (excl. land) from ADQ-specified purchases
supply_chain.cost_to_operate_pct: 25%-35% of operating cost from ADQ-specified purchases

pending_cases.claims: Breach of contract, interference, fraud related to transfer process

guarantee_scope.scope: Joint and several guarantee of full performance of every obligation under the operating agreement
waivers: right to require exhaustion of remedies against licensee
guarantee_scope.survives: Insolvency, bankruptcy, or reorganization of licensee

limitations_on_claims.general: Earlier of: applicable statute of limitations, 1 year from discovery, or 2 years from first act/omission
limitations_on_claims.exceptions: Underreporting Gross Sales, indemnification, and trademark claims — subject only to applicable statutes

california.damages: Liquidated damages may be unenforceable (Civil Code §1671)
california.noncompete: Post-term noncompete may not be enforceable
california.forum_selection: Arbitration venue subject to CA law
california.general_release: Cannot waive Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act rights

illinois.general_release: Release violating IL Franchise Disclosure Act void

maryland.termination: Bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable
maryland.forum_selection: Claims may be brought in Maryland
maryland.general_release: Release does not apply to MD franchise law liability

minnesota.damages: No liquidated damages or termination penalties
minnesota.forum_selection: No out-of-state litigation, no jury trial waiver
minnesota.notice_cure: 90 days termination notice / 60 days cure; 180 days nonrenewal notice
minnesota.general_release: Cannot waive MN franchise law rights

north_dakota.damages: Termination fee provision DELETED IN ITS ENTIRETY
north_dakota.forum_selection: Arbitration at nearest AAA office (not Minneapolis)

rhode_island.forum_selection: Forum restriction outside RI void for RI Act claims

washington.governing_law: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails
washington.general_release: Cannot waive WA Act rights except with independent counsel
washington.transfer: Transfer fees must reflect actual costs
washington.noncompete: Void if employee earns <$100K; no-poach void

unresolveds.description: TOC lists Texas in state addenda but no Texas addendum found in document body. Maryland present but not in TOC.
Only IDQ consolidated financials provided, not ADQ standalone. Buyers cannot evaluate ADQ's standalone financial health.
No FPR — ADQ does not disclose financial performance data. Buyers must rely on independent research.
Item 20 systemwide table start-of-year differs from status table start-of-year (789 vs 785 for 2023). Partially explained by concept conversion footnotes.
Subfranchised 2022 systemwide start (289) vs status table start (389) discrepancy. Unexplained — likely reclassification.

contradictions.description: TOC lists Texas in state addenda; body contains Maryland instead. Document assembly error.
Item 20 systemwide vs status table start-of-year figures differ (789 vs 785 for 2023). Concept conversion footnotes partially explain but do not fully reconcile.
